request.question:
Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.
In plain English: can we engineer baker's yeast to convert a sugar found in agricultural waste into fuel, something it cannot naturally do?

prior_feedback.note: Plasmid Extraction should be divided into multiple steps and account for 3hrs

hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready.

hypothesis.original_hypothesis:
Expressing a heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. in Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK will enable fermentation of xylose to ethanol at a yield of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose within 48 hours under anaerobic conditions, compared to zero ethanol production in the parental strain, due to the enzyme bypassing the native redox-imbalanced xylose reductase/xylitol dehydrogenase pathway.
In plain English: can we engineer baker's yeast to convert a sugar found in agricultural waste into fuel, something it cannot naturally do?

references.title: Metal Dependence of the Xylose Isomerase from Piromyces sp. E2 Explored by Activity Profiling and Protein Crystallography
references.relevance_note: This paper specifically discusses the xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. E2 and its application in Saccharomyces cerevisiae for ethanol production, which is directly relevant to the hypothesis.

plan.objective: To evaluate the ethanol yield from Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK expressing heterologous xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. compared to the parental strain.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment will involve the transformation of Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK with a plasmid encoding the xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. The transformed and parental strains will be cultured in xylose-containing media, and ethanol production will be measured over a defined fermentation period. The ethanol yield will be compared between the two strains to assess the effectiveness of the heterologous enzyme in improving ethanol production.
controls: Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK transformed with a plasmid expressing a validated xylose isomerase.
Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK parental strain cultured in xylose medium without any transformation.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 16 h | Plasmid Preparation
Grow 5 mL of E. coli containing the xylose isomerase plasmid in LB medium with 100 µg/mL ampicillin at 37°C for 16 h. Centrifuge at 4,000 × g for 10 min at room temperature and discard the supernatant.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, 37°C incubator
Reagents: LB medium, 100 µg/mL ampicillin
Expected outcome: E. coli culture reaches an OD600 of 1.5-2.0.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 3 h | Plasmid Extraction
Use the Qiagen Plasmid Mini Kit to extract plasmid DNA from the E. coli pellet. Resuspend the pellet in 250 µL Buffer P1, add 250 µL Buffer P2, and mix gently. Incubate at room temperature for 5 min. Add 350 µL Buffer N3 and centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 10 min. Transfer the supernatant to a spin column and wash with 750 µL Buffer PE. Elute with 50 µL Buffer EB.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, Qiagen Plasmid Mini Kit
Expected outcome: Plasmid DNA is successfully extracted and eluted in Buffer EB.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Plasmid Quantification
Measure the concentration of the extracted plasmid DNA using a Nanodrop spectrophotometer at 260 nm. Record the concentration in ng/µL.
Equipment: Nanodrop ND-1000 spectrophotometer
Expected outcome: Plasmid concentration is determined with a minimum of 100 ng/µL.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 2 h | Yeast Transformation
Transform 100 µL of competent Saccharomyces cerevisiae CEN.PK cells with 1 µg of the xylose isomerase plasmid using the lithium acetate method. Incubate the transformation mixture at 30°C for 30 min, followed by a heat shock at 42°C for 15 min. Recover cells in 1 mL YPD medium for 1 h at 30°C.
Equipment: 30°C incubator, Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge
Expected outcome: Transformation efficiency is confirmed by plating on selective media.

DAY 1 | 2 d | Selection of Transformants
Plate 100 µL of the transformed yeast culture onto YPD agar plates containing 200 µg/mL G418. Incubate at 30°C for 48 h to select for transformants.
Equipment: 30°C incubator
Expected outcome: At least 20 colonies of transformants appear on the selective plates.

DAY 1 | 16 h | Colony Screening
Pick 5 colonies from the selective plates and inoculate each into 5 mL YPD medium with 200 µg/mL G418. Incubate at 30°C for 16 h. Extract plasmid DNA from these cultures to confirm the presence of the xylose isomerase gene.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, Qiagen Plasmid Mini Kit
Expected outcome: At least 3 out of 5 colonies confirm the presence of the xylose isomerase gene by PCR.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Fermentation Setup
Prepare 500 mL of fermentation medium containing 50 g/L xylose, 5 g/L yeast extract, and 5 g/L peptone. Adjust pH to 5.5 using 1 M HCl. Sterilize by autoclaving at 121°C for 15 min.
Equipment: Autoclave, pH meter
Reagents: 50 g/L xylose, 5 g/L yeast extract, 5 g/L peptone, 1 M HCl
Expected outcome: Medium is sterilized and pH adjusted to 5.5.

DAY 1 | 72 h | Inoculation of Fermentation
Inoculate the fermentation medium with 10% (v/v) of the transformed yeast culture and the parental strain. Incubate at 30°C for 72 h with continuous shaking at 200 rpm.
Equipment: 30°C incubator, Orbital shaker
Expected outcome: Fermentation cultures are established with both strains.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Sampling for Ethanol Measurement
At 24, 48, and 72 h, take 1 mL samples from each fermentation culture. Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C to remove cells. Store supernatant at -20°C until analysis.
Equipment: Eppendorf 5424 centrifuge, -20°C freezer
Expected outcome: Supernatants are collected and stored for ethanol analysis.

DAY 3 | 2 h | Ethanol Quantification
Quantify ethanol concentration in the supernatants using gas chromatography (GC) with a Shimadzu GC-2014 system. Inject 1 µL of each sample and analyze using a DB-FFAP column at 80°C for 5 min, then ramp to 200°C at 10°C/min.
Equipment: Shimadzu GC-2014, DB-FFAP column
Expected outcome: Ethanol concentrations are determined with a detection limit of 0.1 g/L.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Data Compilation
Compile ethanol yield data from all time points for both strains. Calculate ethanol yield as grams of ethanol produced per gram of xylose consumed.
Expected outcome: Ethanol yield data is compiled and ready for analysis.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Statistical Analysis
Perform statistical analysis using ANOVA to compare ethanol yields between the transformed and parental strains. Use a significance level of p < 0.05.
Expected outcome: Statistical significance is determined for ethanol yield differences.

DAY 3 | 1 h | Results Interpretation
Interpret the results based on the statistical analysis. Determine if the transformed strain meets the threshold of at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose.
Expected outcome: Conclusions drawn regarding the effectiveness of the heterologous xylose isomerase.
Mitigation step for RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.

assumptions: The xylose isomerase from Piromyces sp. will be successfully expressed in Saccharomyces cerevisiae.
The fermentation conditions will support optimal growth and ethanol production.
Ethanol yield can be accurately quantified using gas chromatography.
decision_criteria: Proceed if ethanol yield from the transformed strain is ≥ 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose.
Proceed if statistical analysis shows p < 0.05 for differences between strains.
Pilot-only mode: do not scale to full run until replicate criteria are met.
risk_mitigations_applied: RISK-001: downgraded to pilot (Downgrade to pilot execution until at least three biological replicates confirm threshold performance.)
RISK-002: Add an explicit process control arm and a pre-run calibration checkpoint to the protocol.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same batch of xylose for all fermentations.
Standardize yeast culture age (24 h) before inoculation.
Calibrate the GC system before each analysis.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 0.4 g ethanol/g xylose improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.
